# Design Taste Profile

*Auto-updated by Claude as references are fed. Do not edit manually.*

---

## Aesthetic Principles

- **Motion has purpose** — every animation communicates state, hierarchy, or feedback. Never decorative-only.
- **Depth through layering** — parallax, glassmorphism, faux-3D via perspective/skew/scale. Flat but with spatial intelligence.
- **Organic over mechanical** — liquid morphs, flowing gradients, claymorphism curves beat rigid transitions.
- **Restraint on ambient motion** — background movement stays subtle; content never competes with animation.
- **Microinteractions as polish** — the quality signal is in hover states, button feedback, form focus. Small things done right.

## Typography

- **Expressive type is valid** — kinetic lettering, glitch effects, self-drawing wordmarks. Type can move.
- **Bold weight for hero** — heavy type with motion > light type with motion. Mass gives animation gravity.
- **Animated wordmarks** — stroke-path self-drawing is the premium treatment for logo typography.

## Color Philosophy

- **Gradients move** — static gradients are floor. Animated gradient shifts that respond to scroll or time are ceiling.
- **Vivid pastels for claymorphism** — oversaturated soft colors (not muted) when using rounded/clay aesthetic.
- **Glassmorphism = low opacity layering** — translucent panels over blurred backgrounds, not white cards.
- **Color as mood over time** — transitions between hues shape emotional arc through the page.

## Motion Principles

- **Scroll-triggered reveals** — content earns its place by appearing when reached, not all at once.
- **Path-based motion** — elements follow intentional trajectories, not just fade/slide.
- **Morphing over cutting** — shapes transform into other shapes. Hard cuts are for video, not UI.
- **Stop-motion for character** — frame-by-frame when you want handmade/nostalgic energy.
- **Microinteractions confirm actions** — every click/hover/submit deserves a response, even a 150ms one.

## Layout & Spacing

- **Hero section carries the animation weight** — above the fold is where motion budget is spent.
- **Bento grid for skeletons** — loading states use structured grid placeholders, not spinners.
- **Horizontal scroll as narrative device** — carousel + scroll-snap for sequential content.
- **Page transitions** — fade or slide between routes, never hard cut.
- **Layered backgrounds** — particles, gradients, patterns animate independently behind content.

## What to Avoid

- Ambient motion that fights content for attention
- Static gradients when animated ones are achievable
- Hard cuts between page states
- Animations that don't respond to user actions (pure decoration)
- Flat everything — depth signals quality

## Style Arsenal

Techniques available to apply on request:

| Technique | When to use |
|-----------|-------------|
| Self-drawing stroke paths | Wordmarks, logo reveals, sketch-style illustrations |
| Morphing shapes | Logo transitions, state changes, liquid UI |
| Parallax layering | Hero sections, storytelling landing pages |
| Scroll-triggered reveals | Article content, feature sections, portfolios |
| Claymorphism | Playful SaaS, consumer apps, anything needing warmth |
| Glassmorphism | Dark UI, layered dashboards, premium/tech feel |
| Neumorphism | Tactile controls, settings UI, minimal light interfaces |
| Isometric animation | Product explainers, data viz, tech illustrations |
| Kinetic typography | Hero headlines, wordmarks, brand statements |
| Animated gradients | Backgrounds, CTAs, hero color fields |
| Microinteractions | Buttons, toggles, form fields, icons — everything tappable |
| Character animation | Brand mascots, explainer content, onboarding |
| Real-time 3D (WebGL) | Product showcases, luxury brands, immersive experiences |
| Mixed media (photo + vector) | Editorial, fashion, lifestyle brands |
| Doodle/hand-drawn | Kids, creative, casual/indie brand energy |

## Raw Observations

### 2026-06-29 — svgator.com/blog/website-animation-examples-and-effects
31 animation examples catalogued. Key patterns:
- Self-drawing SVG paths dominate premium wordmark treatments
- Claymorphism emerging as the "warm tech" aesthetic (vivid pastels + oversized radius + soft shadows)
- Glassmorphism most common in dark/tech UI contexts
- Scroll-triggered animation is table stakes — everyone does it; distinction comes from what triggers and how
- Real-time WebGL rendering (GPU-accelerated) is the high-end frontier for product showcases
- Microinteractions consistently cited as the quality differentiator between amateur and professional UI
- Isometric 2D-as-3D remains versatile for SaaS/product explainers
- Animated gradients used as mood-setting tool, not just decoration
- Mixed media (photo + vector + texture) signals editorial/fashion positioning
